page: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Fasv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with an x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the F48as_v6 demonstrates strong to top-tier performance characteristics. Its multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, ranks in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is also highly competitive, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng and the top 25% for Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with cached small-block read operations reaching the top 10% tier at 7121.77 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write operations fall within the top 25% tier. The processor's instruction set support, including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, contributes to strong performance in encryption and physics simulations.

The instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated physical cores, such as software compilation, image processing, and multi-threaded decompression, all of which scored in the top 10% in benchmarks. It also handles medium-sized language model inference efficiently, achieving top-tier text generation speeds with 7B parameter models. However, because it lacks local storage and GPUs, workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks or hardware-accelerated parallel processing must look elsewhere. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a virtual machine in the Fasv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, particularly for small-block cached reads. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, enabling strong results in software compilation, image processing, and multi-threaded decompression. Given its balanced resource density and high-frequency dedicated cores, the F48as_v6 is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a Fasv6-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 3.7 GHz, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core compute performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48as_v6 is a virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and high-frequency compute performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, dedicated 48-core processor instance with balanced memory, optimized for intensive multi-threaded compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed cached memory bandwidth
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for network-attached Premium Storage
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference
